subject: Public Health
title: Maternal Mortality: A Measure of Inequality
description: A fast breakdown of Maternal Mortality: A Measure of Inequality for builders.

hook.text: A woman in Chad is 200 times more likely to die in childbirth than a woman in Norway.

buildup.text: 287,000 women die each year from pregnancy-related complications—94% in low- and middle-income countries.

discovery.text: Most maternal deaths are preventable—hemorrhage, infection, and high blood pressure have known treatments.

twist.text: In the US, Black women are 3 times more likely to die in childbirth than White women—even with equal income.

climax.text: Skilled birth attendants, emergency obstetric care, and prenatal visits could prevent most maternal deaths.

punchline.text: No woman should die giving life. Yet thousands do—because of where they live.

quiz.question: What percentage of maternal deaths occur in low- and middle-income countries?
